---
title: DHS E-Verify Employment Authorization Attribute Service — Adapter Technical
  Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: dhs-everify-attribute-service
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: identity
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# DHS E-Verify Employment Authorization Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `identity` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The DHS E-Verify Attribute Service Adapter is the **employment-authorization attribute observer** of the UIAO substrate. As a conformance-class identity adapter, it observes employment-authorization attribute-disclosure events from the E-Verify composite authority of record (DHS USCIS + SSA) — both employer-initiated Form I-9 verifications and federal-agency-initiated checks under FAR 52.222-54 — and emits canonical claims with the disclosure-audit envelope.

The adapter is one of a coherent family of federal attribute-service adapters: each wraps a different federal authority of record under a common canonical claim shape. Downstream consumers (POA&M generation, Access Reviews, ATO evidence) do not have to know which federal authority issued an attribute — they read the canonical claim with its provenance envelope and entitlement reference.

Three primary functions:

1. **Disclosure-event observation.** Watches E-Verify case-result events as they are issued — employer-initiated I-9 verifications via the E-Verify web service, federal-agency-initiated checks via the documented FAR 52.222-54 pathway. Each event becomes a canonical claim with the asserted authorization status and the underlying case id.
2. **Disclosure-audit emission.** Every observed disclosure emits a `disclosure-audit` record citing the entitlement (per `reciprocal-consumption-registry.yaml`), the Privacy Act SORN, and the originating case id.
3. **Authority-of-record correlation.** Cross-references the E-Verify response back to the underlying SSA / USCIS authorities of record so downstream consumers can trace each authorization decision to its statutory basis.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-049. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `employment-authorization`, `i9-verification`

**Reads:** E-Verify web service for case results and the documented FAR 52.222-54 federal-agency channel. Authenticated per the E-Verify enrollment agreement; the consuming entitlement is recorded in `canon/reciprocal-consumption-registry.yaml`.

**Emits:** `everify-attribute-response.json` (per-disclosure canonical claim), `everify-disclosure-audit.json` (per-disclosure audit record with entitlement reference and SORN citation), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the IA-2 / IA-4 / AU-2 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** initiate I-9 verifications, access E-Verify case detail beyond what the disclosure event surfaces, store SSN / A-Number values outside the documented retention envelope, or modify the underlying SSA / USCIS authority-of-record systems.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `IA-2`, `IA-4`, `AU-2`, `AU-12`, `SC-8`, `SC-13`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **IA-2** Identification and Authentication | Supporting | Employment-authorization claims supplement identification by establishing the work-eligibility attribute. |
| **IA-4** Identifier Management | Primary | E-Verify case id is the stable identifier for each authorization disclosure. |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Primary | Every disclosure is logged with the canonical disclosure-audit envelope and entitlement reference. |
| **AU-12** Audit Record Generation | Supporting | Audit records carry the canonical provenance envelope per ADR-006. |
| **SC-8** Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity | Primary | All E-Verify exchanges are TLS-protected. |
| **SC-13** Cryptographic Protection | Supporting | Validation uses FIPS-validated cryptographic libraries. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

E-Verify is a composite authority of record (DHS USCIS + SSA) for employment authorization verification under IIRIRA §403. This adapter observes employment-authorization disclosures — both employer-initiated (Form I-9) and federal-agency-initiated (FAR 52.222-54). Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

Member of the federal attribute-service family — pairs with [`uscis-immigration-attribute-service`](../uscis-immigration-attribute-service/uscis-immigration-attribute-service.html), [`ssa-attribute-service`](../ssa-attribute-service/ssa-attribute-service.html), and the other federal authorities-of-record under the canonical claim shape.

ZTMM pillars: identity, data.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-049 — Reserved-slot allocation framework

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
